sections.title: Section I - Multiple Choice
sections.directions:
Section I has 40 multiple-choice questions and lasts 1 hour and 30 minutes.
No points are deducted for incorrect answers. Points are not awarded for unanswered questions.
Determine the answer to each of the following questions or incomplete statements.
Then decide which is the best of the choices given.
Assume that the classes listed in the Java Quick Reference have been imported where appropriate.
Assume that declarations of variables and methods appear within the context of an enclosing class.
Assume that method calls that are not prefixed by an object or class name and are not shown within a complete class definition appear within the context of an enclosing class.
Unless otherwise noted in the question, assume that parameters in method calls are not null and that methods are called only when their preconditions are satisfied.

sections.title: Section II - Free Response
sections.directions:
Section II has 4 free-response questions and lasts 1 hour and 30 minutes.
All program segments must be written in Java. Show all your work. Credit for partial solutions will be given.
Assume that the classes listed in the Java Quick Reference have been imported where appropriate.
Unless otherwise noted in the question, assume that parameters in method calls are not null and that methods are called only when their preconditions are satisfied.
In writing solutions for each question, you may use any of the accessible methods that are listed in classes defined in that question. Writing significant amounts of code that can be replaced by a call to one of these methods will not receive full credit.

questions.prompt: <span aria-label="2. Consider a guessing game in which a player tries to guess a hidden word. The hidden word contains only capital letters and has a length known to the player. A guess contains only capital letters and has the same length as the hidden word. After a guess is made, the player is given a hint that is based on a comparison between the hidden word and the guess. Each position in the hint contains a character that corresponds to the letter in the same position in the guess. The following rules determine the characters that appear in the hint. The figure shows a table with 2 rows and 2 columns. The heading over column one is If the letter in the guess is dot dot dot, the heading over col umn two is the corresponding character in the hint is. The dat a in the table is as fol lows. Row one: Column one, also in the same position in the hidden word comma. Column two, the matching letter. Row two: Column one, also in the hidden word, but in different position comma. Column two, open quote plus close quote. Row three: Column one, not in the hidden word comma. Column two, open quote asterisk close quote. The Hidden Word, initial capital on Hidden and Word and no space between – hence referred to as Hidden Word class will be used to represent the hidden word in the game. The hidden word is passed to the constructor. The class contains a method, get Hint, initial capital on Hint only and no space between – hence referred to as get HiHint, that takes a guess and produces a hint. For example, suppose the variable puzzle is declared as follows. Hidden Word puzzle equals new Hidden Word, open parenthesis, open quote all capital H A R P S close quote, close parenthesis; The following table shows several guesses and the hints that would be produced. The figure shows a table with 5 rows and 2 columns. The heading over column one is Call to get Hint, the heading over c olumn tw o is String Returned. T he data in the table is as follo ws. Row one: Column one, puzzle, dot, get Hint, open parenthesis, open quote all capitals A A A A A close quote, close parenthesis. Column two, open quote plus capital A plus plus plus close quote. Row two: Column one, puzzle, dot, get Hint, open parenthesis, open quote all caps H E L L O close quote, close parenthesis. Column two, open quote capital H asterisk asterisk asterisk asterisk close quote. Row three: Column one, puzzle, dot, get Hint, open parenthesis, open quote all caps H E A R T close quote, close parenthesis. Column two, open quote capital H asterisk plus plus asterisk close quote. Row four: Column one, puzzle, dot, get Hint, open parenthesis, open quote all caps H A R M S close quote, close parenthesis. Column two, open quote all capitals H A R asterisk S close quote. Row five: Column one, puzzle, dot, get Hint, open parenthesis, open quote all caps H A R P S close quote, close parenthesis. Column two, open quote all capitals H A R P S close quote. questions.prompt: <span aria-label="3. A two-dimensional array of integers in which most elements are zero is called a sparse array. Because most elements have a value of zero, memory can be saved by storing only the non-zero values along with their row and column indexes. The following complete Sparse Array Entry, initial capital on Sparse Array and Entry and no space between – hence referred to as Sparse Array Entry class is used to represent non-zero elements in a sparse array. A Sparse Array Entry object cannot be modified after it has been constructed. Moving from top to bottom, the data reads as follows: Row zero: blank, blank, blank, blank , blank Row one: blank, 5, blank, blank , 4 Row two: one, blank, blank, blank , blank Row three: blank, negative 9, blank, blank , blank Row four: blank, blank, blank, blank , blank Row five: blank, blank, blank, blank , blank The sample array can be represented by a Sparse Array object, sparse, with the following instance variable values. Column three: row 3; c o l, one; value, negative 9. Column four: row one; c o l, one; value, 5. open parenaclose paren Write the Sparse Array method get Value At . The method returns the value of the sparse array element at a given row and column in the sparse array. If the list entries contains an entry with the specified row and column, the value associated with the entry is returned. If there is no entry in entries corresponding to the specified row and column, 0 is returned. In the example above, the call sparse dot get Value At open parenthesis 3 comma one close parenthesis would return negative 9, and sparse dot get Value At open parenthesis 3 comma 3 close parenthesis would return 0. constructor that takes two i n t parameters. The first parameter represents the minimum value, and the 
second parameter represents the maximum value of the range. You may assume that the minimum is less 
than or equal to the maximum.
